# DeploymentCookbook realignment as accurate deployment authority

## Context

Sub **F1** of Epic #11720 (Cloud Agent OS Deployment Readiness). **Blocked-by D0 #11721** (realign after the topology decision). MVP-class is a V-B-A item (deferrable-vs-critical — confirm via the sub→PR-chain map).

## The Problem

`learn/agentos/DeploymentCookbook.md` is **stale as deployment authority**: it documents the older clients/proxy/KB/MC/data-layer + two-image-sidecar shape; its only orchestrator section is `Local Orchestrator Dev-Sync Roots` (local-maintainer material, not cloud); its known-gaps list still points at older #108xx work. It misrepresents the current and target Agent OS deployment.

## The Fix

Realign `DeploymentCookbook.md` as accurate deployment authority after D0/D1: the current baseline vs the decided target topology; the cloud-safe scheduler/task taxonomy; the model-provider profile; persistence / bind-mounts; health / proof references; a clearly-isolated **local-only orchestrator appendix** (not the cloud spine); stale known-gaps cleanup.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `DeploymentCookbook.md` reflects the D0-decided topology + the Sub C production reference profile.
- [ ] Local-only orchestrator dev-sync material is isolated into a clearly-labelled appendix.
- [ ] The stale known-gaps list is refreshed (cites the #11720 substrate, not older #108xx work).

## Out of Scope

- The day-0 tutorial (Sub F2 — a separate artifact + ticket).
- #11719 (the `DeploymentCookbook` §6 Markdown table-rendering bug — a separate narrow defect).

## Related

Parent #11720 · blocked-by #11721 (D0) · #11719 (separate `DeploymentCookbook` §6 defect) · Origin Discussion #11718 §5 Sub F1.
